title: APAC Professional Investor Declaration
regulatory_ref: SFC SFO Schedule 1, MAS SFA Section 4A
content:
MERIDIAN WEALTH PARTNERS - APAC PROFESSIONAL INVESTOR DECLARATION

This declaration establishes the framework for identifying and servicing Professional Investors across our Asia-Pacific operations, in compliance with SFC SFO Schedule 1 and MAS SFA Section 4A regulatory requirements.

PROFESSIONAL INVESTOR QUALIFICATION CRITERIA

Under Hong Kong Securities and Futures Ordinance Schedule 1, Professional Investors must satisfy specific financial thresholds. Individual clients require a minimum portfolio of HK$8 million in securities and cash deposits, or single transactions exceeding HK$1 million with aggregate portfolio values of at least HK$8 million. Corporate entities qualify with share capital or net assets exceeding HK$40 million, or managed assets under discretionary investment management of at least HK$40 million.

Singapore's Securities and Futures Act Section 4A defines Accredited Investors with parallel criteria: individuals with net personal assets exceeding S$2 million (excluding primary residence), or annual income of at least S$300,000 in the preceding 12 months. Corporate accredited investors require net assets exceeding S$10 million based on most recent audited financial statements.

CLIENT ONBOARDING OBLIGATIONS

Advisors must obtain written confirmation of Professional Investor status during initial client assessment. Documentation requirements include:

- Audited financial statements for corporate entities
- Bank statements evidencing liquid asset thresholds
- Investment portfolio valuations from recognized custodians
- Income verification through tax returns or employment contracts
- Legal entity documentation for trusts and family offices

Verification must occur annually for individual clients and biennially for institutional counterparts. Clients falling below qualification thresholds require immediate reclassification to retail investor status with corresponding service adjustments.

PRODUCT ACCESS AND LIMITATIONS

Professional Investor classification enables access to restricted investment products including:

- Private equity funds with minimum commitments typically ranging from US$250,000 to US$1 million
- Hedge fund strategies with lock-up periods extending 12-36 months
- Structured products with embedded derivatives exceeding 15% portfolio allocation limits
- Pre-IPO equity positions and secondary market transactions
- Alternative credit instruments including direct lending opportunities

Regulatory capital adequacy requirements mandate Enhanced Due Diligence for transactions exceeding US$500,000 single trade value or representing more than 10% of client's declared net worth.

RISK DISCLOSURE FRAMEWORK

Professional Investors receive modified disclosure treatments acknowledging sophisticated investment knowledge and risk tolerance. Standard retail investor protections including cooling-off periods, simplified risk warnings, and mandatory suitability assessments may be waived subject to explicit written consent.

However, fundamental fiduciary duties remain unchanged. Advisors must maintain comprehensive transaction records, conduct ongoing portfolio monitoring, and provide quarterly performance reporting with benchmark comparisons and fee transparency.

COMPLIANCE MONITORING

Quarterly compliance reviews assess Professional Investor classification accuracy and regulatory adherence. Risk management protocols include position concentration limits, liquidity stress testing, and counterparty exposure monitoring across jurisdictional boundaries.

Violations of Professional Investor criteria or documentation deficiencies trigger immediate remedial action including client re-categorization, product access suspension, and regulatory notification where required.

This declaration requires advisor acknowledgment and annual renewal certification to maintain APAC professional investor servicing authorization.
